Title: TaFT-D1 positively regulates grain weight by acting as a coactivator of TaFDL2 in wheat

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Kenong 199; Chinese Spring; KN9204 (for tafdl2 comparison context)
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major storage carbohydrate contributing to grain filling, grain weight and yield
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Predominantly expressed in flag leaves; not detected at transcript level in grains
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Predominantly in flag leaves; protein detected in developing grains at 15 DPA
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Cytoplasm and nucleus for TaFT-D1(G); cytoplasm only for TaFT-D1(-)
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes grain width, thousand-grain weight, amylose content and total starch content; loss of function reduces grain size and weight and delays heading
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GWAS identified TaFT-D1 as the major candidate underlying variation in thousand-grain weight, heading date and plant height on chromosome 7D. 2. CRISPR knockout of TaFT-D1 reduced grain width and thousand-grain weight and decreased amylose and total starch contents. 3. TaFT-D1 promotes grain development by increasing cell proliferation and starch synthesis, likely as a mobile protein transported from leaves into grains. 4. A natural 1-bp InDel causing frameshift distinguished favorable TaFT-D1(G) and loss-of-function-like TaFT-D1(-) alleles; TaFT-D1(G) was positively selected in Chinese breeding.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS; allele-trait association; CRISPR-Cas9 knockout; RT-qPCR; RNA-seq; proteomics; subcellular localization; microscopy; starch and amylose quantification; protein interaction assays; transient expression assay
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field natural conditions; greenhouse long-day conditions; developing grains sampled at 15 and 20 DPA
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter in transient assays/GFP localization constructs
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: TaFT-D1(G) is associated with earlier heading, shorter plant height, lower spikelet number and grain number per spike than TaFT-D1(-), indicating pleiotropic tradeoffs among yield components and adaptation
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Favorable TaFT-D1(G) allele is useful for improving grain weight and early maturity in Chinese wheat breeding; should be deployed with consideration of tradeoffs on spikelet/grain number and heading date; useful marker-assisted selection target
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts non-enzymatically as a mobile coactivator rather than a transcription factor or metabolic enzyme; natural frameshift allele TaFT-D1(-) loses nuclear localization/interactions and function

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: KN9204; Chinese Spring; Kenong 199 (interaction/transient assays context)
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major storage carbohydrate contributing to grain filling, grain weight and yield
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Predominantly expressed in developing and mature grains
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation; coactivation by TaFT-D1; modulation by 14-3-3A
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Directly binds promoters and activates TaNAC019-3A, TaSWEET15-like-7B, TaCYCD4;1 and TaCYCD3;2
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Starch synthesis and grain-development-associated cell proliferation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator of grain width and thousand-grain weight; tafdl2-1b mutant has smaller grains and lower TGW
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Direct transcriptional activator of downstream cell cycle- and starch synthesis-related genes in the grain-weight regulatory module
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaFDL2 physically interacts with TaFT-D1 and Ta14-3-3A. 2. TaFDL2 is a nucleus-localized transcriptional activator. 3. TaFDL2 directly binds G-box/G-box-like motifs in promoters of TaNAC019-3A, TaSWEET15-like-7B, TaCYCD4;1 and TaCYCD3;2 and activates their expression. 4. Loss of TaFDL2-1B decreases grain width and thousand-grain weight without significantly altering heading date.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis; Y2H; LCI; BiFC; subcellular localization; transcriptional activity assay; Y1H; EMSA; dual-LUC transient expression
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developing grain context; assays in Nicotiana benthamiana and yeast
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 2-kb native promoters of TaNAC019-3A, TaSWEET15-like-7B, TaCYCD4;1, TaCYCD3;2 in dual-LUC assays; 35S effector constructs
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target to improve grain size/weight, potentially through strengthening the TaFT-D1-TaFDL2 module while avoiding heading-date penalties
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: tafdl2-1b carries a premature stop mutation in TaFDL2-1B; TaFDL2 alone activates reporters ~3-4 fold

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major storage carbohydrate contributing to grain filling and grain weight
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed at higher levels than Ta14-3-3B and Ta14-3-3D in all examined tissues
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Broadly expressed; highest among Ta14-3-3 homoeologs in examined tissues
Regulation_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Cell membrane and nucleus when coexpressed with TaFT-D1; nucleus when coexpressed with TaFDL2
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Protein complex assembly/modulation of transcriptional activation
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: TaFDL2 activity toward TaNAC019-3A; TaSWEET15-like-7B; TaCYCD4;1; TaCYCD3;2
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Suppresses TaFDL2-mediated activation of downstream genes unless TaFT-D1 is present
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Regulatory modulation of starch synthesis- and cell proliferation-related gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports formation of the TaFT-D1-Ta14-3-3A-TaFDL2 complex that regulates grain-weight-related downstream genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential interacting component linking TaFT-D1 and TaFDL2 into a functional grain-weight regulatory complex
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression analysis; Y2H; LCI; BiFC; dual-LUC transient expression
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Assays performed in Nicotiana benthamiana and yeast; developing grain regulatory context
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Possible module component for fine-tuning grain-weight pathways, but manipulation may require balancing its inhibitory and complex-forming roles
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The loss-of-function TaFT-D1(-) protein fails to interact with Ta14-3-3A, implicating complex integrity in phenotype
